behavior_summary: Handles the 'U8P_FIRESTRM::hit' event for a fire-stream projectile: establishes event context, prevents further processing for relevant targets, plays a U8P_FIRESTRM::hit sound effect, and destroys the projectile instance.
Acts as a short cleanup-and-feedback routine so the projectile is removed from the world once it has been struck and the player/engine receives audio feedback.
notable_control_flow: Entry on Event 5 (U8P_FIRESTRM::hit) for the fire-stream process (U8P_FIRESTRM::hit).

Sequence: push constant (0x020B) and item instance pointer -> set info (establish event context) -> process exclude (adjust exclusion lists) -> push SFX params -> playSFX -> call Item::destroy on the projectile instance.
No branching or loops observed in this handler; it performs a linear sequence of side-effect operations and exits.
open_questions: What exactly does the constant 0x020B identify in the set info/process exclude sequence (specific info key or reason code)?
What do the playSFX parameters 0x64 and 0x1F represent (volume, variant, priority, or sound id components)?
Are the declared function parameters (source_actor_or_object, hit_value_or_flags) used elsewhere or intentionally unused in this event handler?
Does process exclude target only the projectile or also other actors/items related to the event (scope of exclusion is inferred but not explicit)?

main_function.name: U8P_FIRESTRM::hit
main_function.purpose: On a U8P_FIRESTRM::hit, mark event context and exclusions for the projectile, play a U8P_FIRESTRM::hit SFX for feedback, and destroy the projectile to remove it from the game world.
entry_conditions: Invoked by Event 5 (U8P_FIRESTRM::hit) for the fire-stream instance — triggered when the projectile is struck by an actor or object.

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESTRM::hit
function_map.role: Event handler for the projectile's 'U8P_FIRESTRM::hit' event (cleanup + feedback)

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESTRM::set info
function_map.role: Engine operation to attach event/context metadata to a referenced object (used here to mark or identify the U8P_FIRESTRM::hit context)

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESTRM::process exclude
function_map.role: Engine operation to exclude certain targets from further processing for the current event (prevents duplicate handling)

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESTRM::playSFX
function_map.role: Play a sound effect (called to provide audio feedback on U8P_FIRESTRM::hit)

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: Remove/destroy the item instance representing the projectile

external_calls.purpose: Attach a context identifier (constant 0x020B) and the projectile instance reference to the event so subsequent engine handling knows the origin/context of this U8P_FIRESTRM::hit.
Modify the processing/exclusion list for the current event so specific handlers or the projectile itself are skipped for further processing.
Play a U8P_FIRESTRM::hit sound effect using two byte parameters (0x64, 0x1F) to provide immediate audio feedback for the impact.
Destroy the fire-stream projectile instance to remove it from the world and stop further interactions.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles the 'U8P_FIRESTRM::hit' event for a fire-stream projectile: establishes event context, prevents further processing for relevant targets, plays a U8P_FIRESTRM::hit sound effect, and destroys the projectile instance.
